mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines across rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market health and reduced consumer spending, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.4pp, or the signal loses predictive power during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns, or interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during periods of high inflation indicates supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in this regime.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rates exceeding 4.5 percent impose financing constraints on highly leveraged firms, reducing earnings growth expectations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy in tightening regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in total nonfarm payrolls reflect weakening economic activity and reduced corporate revenue potential, systematically forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS declines fail to predict negative 60-day SPX returns across rolling 3-year windows, or Rank IC remains below 0.03 regardless of economic phase.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion that supports corporate earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined positive momentum of GDPC1 and RSAFS does not precede positive 20-day SPX returns, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for monetary policy shifts.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial output combined with rising consumer prices signals supply-driven inflation that compresses real margins, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns do not turn negative when INDPRO and CPIAUCSL both rise simultaneously, or the mechanism underperforms a baseline strategy in stagflationary periods.
